steps.title: Understand the graph's coordinate system
steps.content: The x-axis represents years after the 2000 census, and the y-axis represents population in thousands. We need to find the population when $x = 2$ (which means 2 years after 2000). Since $y$ is measured in thousands, we'll multiply the graph reading by 1,000 to get the actual population count.

steps.title: Read the population value at x = 2
steps.content: Locate $x = 2$ on the horizontal axis and trace vertically up to the curve. The curve shows $y \approx 4.3$ thousand. Converting to actual population: $4.3 \times 1,000 = 4,300$ people. That matches choice C — C is correct.
